L1.area: API communication protocol parsing and message decoding layer

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the protocol message decoder where addition-based bounds checking arithmetic can wrap around when processing large field length values from a remote client. This allows an attacker with network access to bypass out-of-bounds validation, causing the device to read invalid memory and crash, resulting in denial of service.

L3.area: API component's protobuf wire format decoding and repeated field counting logic
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the protobuf wire format decoder where addition-based bounds checking can wrap around when a malicious client sends a crafted message with a large field length value over the native API port. This bypasses out-of-bounds validation for length-delimited and fixed-width wire types, causing the device to access invalid memory and crash.

ground_truth.reason: The bounds check `ptr + field_length > end` in the protobuf decoder can overflow when field_length is large, bypassing the out-of-bounds check and causing the device to read invalid memory and crash.
